description_long: This is a round, green cabbage with a tightly packed head composed of overlapping, textured leaves. The surface displays a prominent network of veins, giving the leaves a crinkled and rugged appearance. The coloration ranges from pale green on the outermost leaves to a deeper, richer green toward the center. The leaf margins are slightly wavy, and the overall form is nearly spherical, with no visible stem or root. The cabbage appears fresh, with no signs of wilting or damage, and the leaves are closely layered, forming a dense, solid mass. This type of cabbage is commonly found in markets, grocery stores, or urban gardens.
description: A round, green cabbage with tightly overlapping, crinkled leaves and a dense, compact head. The surface is textured with visible veins and varying shades of green.
description_view_0: This view shows the cabbage from a slightly angled side, highlighting the overlapping, veined leaves and a subtle seam where two leaf sections meet.
description_view_1: This view presents the cabbage from above, emphasizing the central leaf vein and the intricate, crinkled texture of the surface.
description_view_2: This view displays the cabbage from another side, showing a prominent central vein and the layered, wavy leaf edges.
description_view_3: This view reveals the cabbage from a more rounded angle, focusing on the smooth curvature and the dense, compact arrangement of the leaves.
